title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks in R
text: rkaf R package Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks using torch backend. package provides modern R interface KAF models, including: regression binary classification multiclass classification formula matrix interfaces mini-batch training validation splits early stopping automatic standardization best-model restoration KAF-specific diagnostics goal rkaf make KAF-style neural networks accessible R users without requiring Python, reticulate, custom training loops.

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks in R
text: can install development version locally : local package directory: rkaf depends torch. torch already configured, run:
code: devtools::install() devtools::load_all() torch::install_torch()

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks in R
code: library(rkaf)  set.seed(123) torch::torch_manual_seed(123)  x <- as.matrix(seq(-1, 1, length.out = 128))  y <- sin(8 * pi * x) +   0.35 * cos(3 * pi * x) +   0.15 * x^2  fit <- kaf_fit(   x = x,   y = y,   hidden = c(32, 32),   num_grids = 8,   use_layernorm = FALSE,   epochs = 100,   lr = 1e-3,   verbose = FALSE,   seed = 123 )  fit #> <kaf_fit> #> Task:         regression #> Architecture: 1 -> 32 -> 32 -> 1 #> Fourier grids: 8  #> Epochs:       100 #> Batch size:   128 #> Validation:   no #> Standardize x: yes  #> Standardize y: no  #> Final loss:   0.546906 #> Best loss:    0.546906 at epoch 100 pred <- predict(fit, x)  plot(   x,   y,   type = "l",   lwd = 2,   xlab = "x",   ylab = "f(x)",   main = "KAF regression example" )  lines(x, pred, lwd = 2, lty = 2)  legend(   "topright",   legend = c("Observed", "Predicted"),   lty = c(1, 2),   lwd = 2,   bty = "n" )

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks in R
text: rkaf also supports formula interface tabular data.
code: fit_mtcars <- kaf_fit_formula(   mpg ~ wt + hp + cyl,   data = mtcars,   hidden = c(16, 16),   num_grids = 8,   epochs = 50,   verbose = FALSE,   seed = 123 )  fit_mtcars #> <kaf_fit> #> Task:         regression #> Formula:      mpg ~ wt + hp + cyl #> Architecture: 3 -> 16 -> 16 -> 1 #> Fourier grids: 8  #> Epochs:       50 #> Batch size:   32 #> Validation:   no #> Standardize x: yes  #> Standardize y: no  #> Final loss:   408.972 #> Best loss:    408.972 at epoch 50 head(predict(fit_mtcars, mtcars)) #> [1] 0.7024449 0.7028257 0.6777840 0.7032931 0.8980367 0.7056480

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks in R
text: factor, character, logical targets two classes, rkaf can fit binary classifier automatically. Predicted probabilities: Predicted classes:
code: df <- mtcars df$high_mpg <- factor(   ifelse(df$mpg > median(df$mpg), "yes", "no"),   levels = c("no", "yes") )  fit_binary <- kaf_fit_formula(   high_mpg ~ wt + hp + cyl,   data = df,   hidden = c(16, 16),   num_grids = 8,   epochs = 50,   verbose = FALSE,   seed = 123 )  fit_binary #> <kaf_fit> #> Task:         binary #> Classes:      no, yes #> Formula:      high_mpg ~ wt + hp + cyl #> Architecture: 3 -> 16 -> 16 -> 1 #> Fourier grids: 8  #> Epochs:       50 #> Batch size:   32 #> Validation:   no #> Standardize x: yes  #> Standardize y: no  #> Final loss:   0.571078 #> Best loss:    0.571078 at epoch 50 head(predict(fit_binary, df, type = "prob")) #> [1] 0.5526212 0.5515673 0.5633891 0.5482979 0.4112424 0.5429556 head(predict(fit_binary, df, type = "class")) #> [1] yes yes yes yes no  yes #> Levels: no yes

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks in R
text: factor targets two classes, rkaf fits multiclass model. Class probabilities: Predicted classes:
code: fit_iris <- kaf_fit_formula(   Species ~ .,   data = iris,   hidden = c(16, 16),   num_grids = 8,   epochs = 50,   verbose = FALSE,   seed = 123 )  fit_iris #> <kaf_fit> #> Task:         multiclass #> Classes:      setosa, versicolor, virginica #> Formula:      Species ~ . #> Architecture: 4 -> 16 -> 16 -> 3 #> Fourier grids: 8  #> Epochs:       50 #> Batch size:   150 #> Validation:   no #> Standardize x: yes  #> Standardize y: no  #> Final loss:   0.89159 #> Best loss:    0.89159 at epoch 50 head(predict(fit_iris, iris, type = "prob")) #>         setosa versicolor virginica #> [1,] 0.4097730  0.3381031 0.2521239 #> [2,] 0.3848806  0.3663312 0.2487882 #> [3,] 0.3924690  0.3590669 0.2484642 #> [4,] 0.3879960  0.3633747 0.2486294 #> [5,] 0.4149875  0.3291803 0.2558322 #> [6,] 0.4292243  0.3035112 0.2672646 head(predict(fit_iris, iris, type = "class")) #> [1] setosa setosa setosa setosa setosa setosa #> Levels: setosa versicolor virginica

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks in R
text: kaf_fit() supports validation splits, mini-batching, early stopping.
code: fit_val <- kaf_fit(   x = x,   y = y,   hidden = c(32, 32),   num_grids = 8,   use_layernorm = FALSE,   epochs = 300,   batch_size = 32,   validation_split = 0.2,   patience = 30,   verbose = FALSE,   seed = 123 )  fit_val #> <kaf_fit> #> Task:         regression #> Architecture: 1 -> 32 -> 32 -> 1 #> Fourier grids: 8  #> Epochs:       31 #> Batch size:   32 #> Validation:   yes #> Standardize x: yes  #> Standardize y: no  #> Stopped at:   31 #> Final loss:   0.506043 #> Best loss:    0.734597 at epoch 1 plot(fit_val)

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks in R
text: can inspect learned balance base/GELU branch Fourier branch.

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks in R
text: Advanced users can directly create KAF torch modules.
code: model <- nn_kaf(   layers = c(4, 16, 16, 1),   num_grids = 8 )  x_tensor <- torch::torch_randn(10, 4) y_tensor <- model(x_tensor)  y_tensor$shape #> [1] 10  1

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks in R
text: package currently includes: full test coverage core API, including regression, binary classification, multiclass classification, formula handling, prediction, training utilities, diagnostics.
code: devtools::test() devtools::check()

what: Create a Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — kaf
title: Create a Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — kaf
text: Convenience wrapper around nn_kaf() using user-facing API.

title: Create a Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — kaf
code: kaf(   input_dim,   output_dim = 1,   hidden = c(64, 64),   num_grids = 16,   dropout = 0,   use_layernorm = TRUE,   activation_expectation = 1.64,   fourier_init_scale = 0.01 )

title: Create a Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — kaf
text: input_dim Integer. Number input features. output_dim Integer. Number output dimensions. hidden Integer vector. Hidden layer sizes. num_grids Integer. Number Fourier frequencies per KAF layer. dropout Numeric. Dropout probability. use_layernorm Logical. Whether apply layer normalization. activation_expectation Numeric. Scaling constant Fourier initialization. fourier_init_scale Numeric. Initial scale Fourier branch.

title: Create a Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — kaf
text: torch KAF network.

what: Fit a Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — kaf_fit
title: Fit a Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — kaf_fit
text: Fits KAF model regression using mean squared error loss.

title: Fit a Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — kaf_fit
code: kaf_fit(   x,   y,   task = c("auto", "regression", "binary", "multiclass"),   hidden = c(64, 64),   num_grids = 16,   dropout = 0,   use_layernorm = TRUE,   fourier_init_scale = 0.01,   epochs = 1000,   lr = 0.001,   batch_size = NULL,   shuffle = TRUE,   validation_split = 0,   x_val = NULL,   y_val = NULL,   weight_decay = 0,   standardize_x = TRUE,   standardize_y = FALSE,   patience = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   print_every = 100,   seed = NULL,   restore_best = TRUE,   min_delta = 0 )

title: Fit a Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — kaf_fit
text: x Matrix, data frame, vector, 2D torch tensor predictors. y Vector, matrix, data frame, torch tensor targets. task Character. One "auto", "regression", "binary", "multiclass". "auto", factor, character, logical targets treated classification; numeric targets treated regression. hidden Integer vector. Hidden layer sizes. num_grids Integer. Number Fourier frequencies per KAF layer. dropout Numeric. Dropout probability. use_layernorm Logical. Whether apply layer normalization. fourier_init_scale Numeric. Initial scale Fourier branch. epochs Integer. Maximum number training epochs. lr Numeric. Learning rate. batch_size Optional integer. Mini-batch size. NULL, full-batch training used. shuffle Logical. Whether shuffle training rows epoch. validation_split Numeric [0, 1). Fraction rows reserve validation. Ignored x_val y_val supplied. x_val Optional validation predictors. y_val Optional validation targets. weight_decay Numeric. Adam weight decay. standardize_x Logical. Whether standardize predictors using training-set mean standard deviation. standardize_y Logical. Whether standardize regression targets using training-set mean standard deviation. Predictions automatically transformed back original target scale. patience Optional integer. Number epochs without improvement early stopping. verbose Logical. Whether print progress. print_every Integer. Print frequency. seed Optional integer random seed. restore_best Logical. Whether restore best observed model state training. min_delta Numeric. Minimum loss improvement required update best model state.

title: Fit a Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — kaf_fit
text: object class "kaf_fit".

title: Fit a KAF model using an R formula — kaf_fit_formula
text: formula model formula, y ~ x1 + x2. data data frame. include_intercept Logical. Whether keep model-matrix intercept column. Defaults FALSE. na.action Missing-value handling function passed model.frame(). ... Additional arguments passed kaf_fit().

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — nn_kaf
text: Torch module implementing stacked Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network.

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — nn_kaf
code: nn_kaf(   layers,   num_grids = 8,   dropout = 0,   use_layernorm = TRUE,   activation_expectation = 1.64,   fourier_init_scale = 0.01 )

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — nn_kaf
text: layers Integer vector. Network architecture, including input output dimensions. example, c(10, 64, 64, 1). num_grids Integer. Number Fourier frequencies per KAF layer. dropout Numeric. Dropout probability. use_layernorm Logical. Whether apply layer normalization Fourier feature block. activation_expectation Numeric. Scaling constant Fourier initialization. fourier_init_scale Numeric. Initial scale Fourier component.

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — nn_kaf
text: torch nn_module.

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Layer — nn_kaf_layer
text: Torch module implementing one KAF layer using hybrid GELU trainable Random Fourier Features activation.

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Layer — nn_kaf_layer
code: nn_kaf_layer(   input_dim,   output_dim,   num_grids = 8,   dropout = 0,   use_layernorm = TRUE,   activation_expectation = 1.64,   fourier_init_scale = 0.01 )

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Layer — nn_kaf_layer
text: input_dim Integer. Input dimension. output_dim Integer. Output dimension. num_grids Integer. Number Fourier frequencies. dropout Numeric. Dropout probability. use_layernorm Logical. Whether apply layer normalization Fourier feature block. activation_expectation Numeric. Scaling constant Fourier initialization. fourier_init_scale Numeric. Initial scale Fourier component.

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Layer — nn_kaf_layer
text: torch nn_module.

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Layer — nn_kaf_layer
text: layer computes feature-wise hybrid activation form: $$ z = \alpha \odot GELU(x) + \beta \odot RFF(x) $$ followed linear projection output dimension.

title: Random Fourier Features Layer — nn_random_fourier_features
text: Torch module implementing trainable random Fourier features Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks.

title: Random Fourier Features Layer — nn_random_fourier_features
code: nn_random_fourier_features(   input_dim,   num_grids = 8,   dropout = 0,   activation_expectation = 1.64 )

title: Random Fourier Features Layer — nn_random_fourier_features
text: input_dim Integer. Input dimension. num_grids Integer. Number Fourier frequencies. dropout Numeric. Dropout probability. activation_expectation Numeric. Scaling constant initialization.

title: Random Fourier Features Layer — nn_random_fourier_features
text: torch nn_module.

title: Predict from a fitted KAF model — predict.kaf_fit
text: object fitted object returned kaf_fit(). newdata Matrix, data frame, vector, 2D torch tensor. type Character. Prediction type. "response" returns regression predictions regression, probabilities classification. "prob" returns probabilities classification. "class" returns predicted classes. "link" returns raw model logits/outputs. threshold Numeric. Classification threshold used binary class predictions. as_tensor Logical. TRUE, return torch tensor supported. ... Unused.
